channel.title: 2026-06-22 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-06-22 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Fast native three-qubit gates and fault-tolerant quantum error correction with trapped Rydberg ions
item.description:
📚 Quantum Science and Technology
👤 Katrin Bolsmann, Thiago L M Guedes, Weibin Li, Joseph W P Wilkinson, Igor Lesanovsky and Markus Müller
Trapped ions are one of the most promising quantum-information-processing platforms, yet conventional entangling gates mediated by collective motion remain slow and difficult to scale. Exciting trapped ions to high-lying electronic Rydberg states provides a promising route to overcome these limitations by enabling strong, long-range dipole–dipole interactions that support much faster multi-qubit operations. Here, we introduce the first scheme for implementing a native controlled–controlled-Z gate with microwave-dressed Rydberg ions by optimizing a single-pulse protocol that accounts for the finite Rydberg-state lifetime. Under realistic conditions, the resulting gate achieves fidelities above with execution times of about at cryogenic temperatures, making it more than faster than standard…

item.title: Universality in the Anticoncentration of Noisy Quantum Circuits at Finite Depths
item.description:
📚 PRX Quantum
👤 Arman Sauliere, Guglielmo Lami, Corentin Boyer, Jacopo De Nardis, and Andrea De Luca
Author(s): Arman Sauliere, Guglielmo Lami, Corentin Boyer, Jacopo De Nardis, and Andrea De Luca A universal description of noisy random circuits reveals depth-dependent regimes and architecture-independent anticoncentration. [PRX Quantum 7, 020365] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Identifying the three-qubit W state with the quantum uncertainty relation
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Zhi-Jie Liu, Hao-Nan Qiang, Jie Zhou, Mi Xie, and Jing-Ling Chen
Author(s): Zhi-Jie Liu, Hao-Nan Qiang, Jie Zhou, Mi Xie, and Jing-Ling Chen The W state, a canonical representative of multipartite quantum entanglement, plays a crucial role in quantum information science due to its robust entanglement properties. Quantum uncertainty relations, on the other hand, form a fundamental cornerstone of quantum mechanics. This work introduces a… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062451] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Microwave-enabled continuous-variable measurement-device-independent quantum key distribution in free space
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Zhuokuan Jia, Tianai Zhou, Yongxin Feng, and Ying Guo
Author(s): Zhuokuan Jia, Tianai Zhou, Yongxin Feng, and Ying Guo Continuous-variable quantum key distribution (CV-QKD) over free-space channels offers more flexibility for secure key exchange between authenticated parties than fiber-based CV-QKD in practical scenarios. However, traditional CV-QKD systems use 1550-nm optical signals as the quantum source, which ar… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062450] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Quantum simulation of general spin-frac12 Hamiltonians with parity-violating fermionic Gaussian states
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Michael Kaicher, Joseph Vovrosh, Alexandre Dauphin, and Simon B. Jäger
Author(s): Michael Kaicher, Joseph Vovrosh, Alexandre Dauphin, and Simon B. Jäger We introduce equations of motion for a parity-violating fermionic mean-field theory (PV-FMFT): a numerically efficient fermionic mean-field theory based on parity-violating fermionic Gaussian states (PV-FGS). This work provides explicit equations of motion for studying the real- and imaginary-time e… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 245140] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Rényi-like entanglement probe of the chiral central charge
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Julian Gass and Michael Levin
Author(s): Julian Gass and Michael Levin We propose a ground-state entanglement probe for gapped, two-dimensional quantum many-body systems that involves taking powers of reduced density matrices in a particular geometric configuration. This quantity, which we denote by ωα,β, is parameterized by two positive real numbers, αphantom… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235136] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Tensor network loop cluster expansions for quantum many-body problems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Johnnie Gray, Gunhee Park, Glen Evenbly, Nicola Pancotti, Eirik F. Kjønstad, and Garnet Kin-Lic Chan
Author(s): Johnnie Gray, Gunhee Park, Glen Evenbly, Nicola Pancotti, Eirik F. Kjønstad, and Garnet Kin-Lic Chan We analyze the tensor network loop cluster expansion, introduced by G. Park et al. [ Phys. Rev. B 112 , 174310 (2025) ] as a systematic correction to belief propagation, in the context of general quantum many-body problems. We provide numerical examples of the accuracy and practical applicability of t… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235135] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Sensitivity enhancement in superconducting-qubit magnetometry via two-level systems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Azat Gubaydullin, Vladimir Slepnev, Valerii Vinokur, and Florian Neukart
Author(s): Azat Gubaydullin, Vladimir Slepnev, Valerii Vinokur, and Florian Neukart We study quantum magnetometry based on phase-estimation algorithms in a sensor formed by a superconducting qubit coupled to a two-level system (TLS). Using Ramsey interferometry with single-shot binary readout, we construct a precomputed calibration pattern P₀(fq,τ,ϕₘ) and implement… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224522] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Zeeman-like coupling to valley degree of freedom in Si-based spin qubits
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 S. A. Jafari, Hendrik Bluhm, and David P. DiVincenzo
Author(s): S. A. Jafari, Hendrik Bluhm, and David P. DiVincenzo Increasing the valley splitting in Si-based heterostructures is critical for improving the performance of semiconductor qubits. This paper establishes that the two low-energy conduction band valleys are not independent parabolic bands. Instead, they originate from the X point of the Brillouin zone, … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 245204] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Logarithmic growth of operator entanglement in a clean nonintegrable circuit
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Mao Tian Tan and Tomaž Prosen
Author(s): Mao Tian Tan and Tomaž Prosen We study a so-called semiergodic brickwork dual-unitary circuit where, in the infinite volume limit, the two-point correlation functions of single-site operators exhibit ergodic behavior along one light ray and nonergodic behavior along the other light ray. Here, however, we study intermediate and l… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224316] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Triggering and switching of topological phases and non-Hermitian transitions in a Su-Schrieffer-Heeger circuit with long-range hopping and synthetic gauge fields
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Jing Cheng, Cheng-Jun Han, Jin-Yan Hu, Mo-Nan Wang, and Y. P. Wang
Author(s): Jing Cheng, Cheng-Jun Han, Jin-Yan Hu, Mo-Nan Wang, and Y. P. Wang We propose an extended Su-Schrieffer-Heeger chain that integrates long-range hopping and a synthetic Peierls phase, providing a tunable circuit platform to trigger and control non-Hermitian transitions and topological phase transitions. We show that the long-range hopping strength acts as a trigger … [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063732] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Gradient scalability and Taylor surrogation of quantum cost landscapes
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Sabri Meyer, Francesco Scala, Francesco Tacchino, and Aurelien Lucchi
Author(s): Sabri Meyer, Francesco Scala, Francesco Tacchino, and Aurelien Lucchi Variational quantum algorithms (VQAs) are promising candidates for near-term quantum computing, yet they face scalability challenges due to barren plateaus, where gradients vanish exponentially in the system size. Recent conjectures suggest that avoiding barren plateaus might inherently lead to clas… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023325] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Hexapartite active nonlinear feedback control in four-wave mixing with a spatially structured pump
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Guanjun Zeng, Xiaozhou Pan, Kai Zhang, and Jietai Jing
Author(s): Guanjun Zeng, Xiaozhou Pan, Kai Zhang, and Jietai Jing In the field of quantum information science, feedback control has become an important method for preparing and manipulating nonclassical beams. Here we theoretically propose an active nonlinear feedback control system utilizing two four-wave-mixing processes FWM₁ and FWM₂… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062448] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Noise-aware time-optimal quantum control
item.description:
📚 Quantum Science and Technology
👤 Minjun Jeon and Zhenyu Cai
Quantum optimal control plays a vital role in many quantum technologies, including quantum computation. One of the most important control parameters to optimise for is the evolution time (pulse duration). However, most existing works focus on finding the shortest evolution time theoretically possible without offering explicit pulse constructions under practical constraints like noise in the system. This paper addresses these limitations by introducing an efficient method to perform the chopped random basis optimisation in the presence of noise, specifically when the noise commutes with the gate Hamiltonian, i.e. when the unitary and dissipative parts of the Liouvillian commute. This noise-aware approach allows for direct optimisation of the evolution time alongside other control parameter…

item.title: Higher-order adiabatic elimination in atom-cavity systems and its impact on spin-squeezing generation
item.description:
📚 Quantum Science and Technology
👤 Stefano Giaccari, Giulia Dellea, Marco G Genoni and Gianluca Bertaina
Spin-squeezed states are metrologically useful quantum states where entanglement allows for enhanced sensing with respect to the standard quantum limit. Key challenges include the efficient preparation of spin-squeezed states and the scalability of estimation precision with the number of probes. Recently, in the context of the generation of spin-squeezed states via coupling of three-level atoms to an optical cavity, it was shown that increasing the atom-cavity coupling can be detrimental to spin squeezing generation, an effect that is not captured by the standard second-order adiabatic cavity removal approximation. We describe adiabatic elimination techniques to derive an effective Lindblad master equation up to third order for the atomic degrees of freedom. Numerical simulations show tha…

item.title: Equilibrium thermometry in the multilevel quantum Rabi model
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Tabitha Doicin, Luis A. Correa, Jonas Glatthard, Andrew D. Armour, and Gerardo Adesso
Author(s): Tabitha Doicin, Luis A. Correa, Jonas Glatthard, Andrew D. Armour, and Gerardo Adesso The temperature sensitivity of a probe in equilibrium can be gauged by its thermal quantum Fisher information (QFI). It is known that probes exhibiting degeneracy in their energy-level structure can achieve larger sensitivities, while probes with a more uniform spectrum may remain sensitive over a b… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062449] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: One-particle density matrix framework for mode-shell correspondence: Characterizing topology in amorphous higher-order topological insulators
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Miguel F. Martínez, Lucien Jezequel, Jens H. Bardarson, Thomas Klein Kvorning, and Julia D. Hannukainen
Author(s): Miguel F. Martínez, Lucien Jezequel, Jens H. Bardarson, Thomas Klein Kvorning, and Julia D. Hannukainen We present a framework for characterizing higher-order topological phases directly from the one-particle density matrix, without any reference to an underlying Hamiltonian. Our approach extends the mode-shell correspondence, originally formulated for single-particle Hamiltonians, to Gaussian states … [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023320] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Optimal work extraction from finite-time closed quantum dynamics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Shoki Sugimoto, Takahiro Sagawa, and Ryusuke Hamazaki
Author(s): Shoki Sugimoto, Takahiro Sagawa, and Ryusuke Hamazaki Extracting useful work from quantum systems is a fundamental problem in quantum thermodynamics. In scenarios where rapid protocols are desired—whether due to practical constraints or deliberate design choices—a fundamental trade-off between power and efficiency is yet to be established. Here, we inv… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023321] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Quantum dynamical signatures of topological flow transitions in limit cycle phases
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Alejandro S. Gómez and Javier del Pino
Author(s): Alejandro S. Gómez and Javier del Pino Quantum self-oscillatory phases are ubiquitous in driven-dissipative systems. Classically, each phase is defined by its flow pattern and how stationary sets organize phase space (e.g., fixed points and limit cycles), with transitions triggered by local bifurcations or global basin rearrangements. In… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023319] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Dielectric screening in electromagnetic dressing of semiconductors
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Quentin Courtade, Umberto Dellasette, Sotirios Fragkos, Stéphane Petit, Dominique Descamps, Yann Mairesse, and Samuel Beaulieu
Author(s): Quentin Courtade, Umberto Dellasette, Sotirios Fragkos, Stéphane Petit, Dominique Descamps, Yann Mairesse, and Samuel Beaulieu Nonequilibrium manipulation of quantum materials via electromagnetic dressing provides an on-demand route to tailoring electronic band structures through Floquet engineering. Time- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy offers a direct means to probe these light-dressed electronic states. In … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235430] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Quantum states supported by matroids
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Xiaowei Huang, Fei Shi, Lijun Zhang, and Lvzhou Li
Author(s): Xiaowei Huang, Fei Shi, Lijun Zhang, and Lvzhou Li In this work, we establish a structural correspondence between quantum states and matroid theory. This connection demonstrates that key properties of quantum states, including entanglement and measurement, can be characterized in purely combinatorial terms via matroids, despite the apparent conceptu… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062452] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Atomistic approach to exciton-phonon couplings in semiconductor quantum dots
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Yasser Saleem and Moritz Cygorek
Author(s): Yasser Saleem and Moritz Cygorek We present a fully atomistic approach to exciton-phonon coupling in semiconductor quantum dots that bridges microscopic electronic-structure calculations with non-Markovian open-quantum-system dynamics. On the example of an InAsP quantum dot embedded in an InP matrix, we compute single-particle stat… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235139] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Control framework for broadband population transfer in multilevel quantum ensembles with time-dependent dispersions
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Vishesh Kaushik and Navin Khaneja
Author(s): Vishesh Kaushik and Navin Khaneja We develop a control framework for broadband population transfer in ensembles of n-level quantum systems with time-dependent energy levels and time-varying dispersion across ensemble members. Ensemble inhomogeneity is modeled through time-varying dispersion functions that capture Hamiltonian varia… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062620] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Edge state effects in quantum work statistics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Moallison F. Cavalcante
Author(s): Moallison F. Cavalcante Motivated by the objective of quantifying the energetic cost of accessing boundary phases through local control, we investigate here a simple, analytically tractable quantum impurity model. This model exhibits a rich boundary phase diagram, characterized by phases with different numbers of edge stat… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214321] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Finite-size effects on the edge loss probability in non-Hermitian quantum walks
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Shuaixian Liu, Yulan Dong, Bowen Zeng, and Meng-Qiu Long
Author(s): Shuaixian Liu, Yulan Dong, Bowen Zeng, and Meng-Qiu Long A dynamical bulk-edge relation in quantum walks has been theoretically proposed and experimentally observed, in which a power-law dependence of the bulk loss probability is associated with a pronounced peak of loss probability at the edge. This behavior has been proven to arise from imaginary gap cl… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214319] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Observing fundamental triple uncertainty relations
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Yan Wang, Jie Zhou, Xing-Yan Fan, Ze-Yan Hao, Jia-Kun Li, Zheng-Hao Liu, Ya Xiao, Kai Sun, Jin-Shi Xu, Jing-Ling Chen, Chuan-Feng Li, and Guang-Can Guo
Author(s): Yan Wang, Jie Zhou, Xing-Yan Fan, Ze-Yan Hao, Jia-Kun Li, Zheng-Hao Liu, Ya Xiao, Kai Sun, Jin-Shi Xu, Jing-Ling Chen, Chuan-Feng Li, and Guang-Can Guo The uncertainty principle, arising from the incompatibility of noncommuting observables, constitutes one of the cornerstones of quantum mechanics. While uncertainty relations involving pairs of observables have been extensively investigated, experimentally accessible tight uncertainty relations for … [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062447] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Quantum theory for edge current and noise in two-dimensional topological superconductors
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 S. Pintus and A. Crépieux
Author(s): S. Pintus and A. Crépieux We calculate the edge current and its fluctuations, i.e., noise, in a two-dimensional topological superconductor using the T-matrix and the Green's function techniques. We show that the current is zero for nonchiral edge states and nonzero for chiral edge states, while the edge noise is nonzero wh… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235428] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Record-high Glass coefficient in the shift current response of a ferroelectric halide perovskite
item.description:
📚 PNAS
👤 Koma MikiMasao NakamuraAsahi YamadaGurvan BosserKiyohiro AdachiDaisuke HashizumeNaoki OgawaSatoshi OkamotoYoshinori TokuraMasashi Kawasakiahttps://ror.org/057zh3y96Department of Applied Physics and Quantum-Phase Electronics Center, The University of Tokyo, Tokyo 113-8656, Japanbhttps://ror.org/057zh3y96Department of Fundamental Engineering, Institute of Industrial Science, The University of Tokyo, Tokyo 153-8505, Japanchttps://ror.org/03gv2xk61RIKEN Center for Emergent Matter Science, Wako 351-0198, JapandRIKEN Baton Zone Program, Wako 351-0198, Japanehttps://ror.org/01dq60k83Department of Physics, Graduate School of Science, Tohoku University, Sendai 980-8578, JapanfSumitomo Chemical Co., Ltd, Tokyo 103-6020, Japanghttps://ror.org/057zh3y96Tokyo College, The University of Tokyo, Tokyo 113-8656, Japan
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 26, June 2026. SignificanceShift current is a second-order nonlinear optical effect arising from the change in the quantum geometry of wave functions, and provides a photovoltaic mechanism fundamentally distinct from conventional junction-based photovoltaics. CsGeI3is ...

item.title: Temperature-driven false-vacuum decay in coherently coupled Bose superfluids
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Paniyanchatha Moolayil Sivasankar, Franco Dalfovo, Alessio Recati, and Arko Roy
Author(s): Paniyanchatha Moolayil Sivasankar, Franco Dalfovo, Alessio Recati, and Arko Roy The relaxation of a quantum field from a metastable state (false vacuum) to a stable one (true vacuum), also known as false-vacuum decay, is a fundamental problem in quantum field theory and cosmology. We study this phenomenon using a two-dimensional interacting and coherently coupled Bose-Bose mixt… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063323] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Theory of high-harmonic generation with quantum light
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Haoyu Liu, Hanxu Zhang, Xu Wang, and Jianmin Yuan
Author(s): Haoyu Liu, Hanxu Zhang, Xu Wang, and Jianmin Yuan High-harmonic generation (HHG) driven by intense quantum light has recently emerged as a new frontier in strong-field physics. Yet, the mechanism underlying HHG with nonclassical fields such as bright squeezed vacuum (BSV) light—characterized by a vanishing mean electric field—remains elusive. In th… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023326] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Topological and nontopological defects from quantum reset dynamics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 R. Jafari, Henrik Johannesson, and Sebastian Eggert
Author(s): R. Jafari, Henrik Johannesson, and Sebastian Eggert We analyze mechanisms for universal out-of-equilibrium dynamics near criticality by exploring the effect of randomized quantum resetting (QR) under a linear finite-time drive across, or very close to, an equilibrium quantum critical point. Using the transverse-field Ising chain as a minimal model an… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224312] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Unconventional distance scaling of Casimir-Polder force between atomic arrays
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Qihang Ye, Haosheng Fu, Bing Miao, and Lei Ying
Author(s): Qihang Ye, Haosheng Fu, Bing Miao, and Lei Ying Conventionally, dispersion forces mediated by quantum vacuum fluctuations are known to exhibit universal distance scaling, with retardation typically leading to a faster decay of the interaction. Here, we show that this expectation fails for intrinsically discrete systems. Using the microscopic scat… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062813] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Zeeman-activated Berry-curvature magnetotransport from the bulk of nonmagnetic metals with inversion symmetry
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Rhonald Burgos Atencia, Antonio Vecchione, Denys Makarov, and Carmine Ortix
Author(s): Rhonald Burgos Atencia, Antonio Vecchione, Denys Makarov, and Carmine Ortix The Berry curvature (BC)—a quantity encoding the geometry of electronic wavefunctions—governs various electronic transport effects in quantum materials. In magnetic systems, the BC is reponsible for the intrinsic part of the anomalous Hall conductivity. Local concentrations of BC in noncentrosymmetr… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235134] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Process Tensor Approaches to Non-Markovian Quantum Dynamics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. X
👤 Jonathan Keeling, E. Miles Stoudenmire, Mari-Carmen Bañuls, and David R. Reichman
Author(s): Jonathan Keeling, E. Miles Stoudenmire, Mari-Carmen Bañuls, and David R. Reichman Researchers review the process tensor framework and demonstrate how efficient tensor-network representations enable the practical simulation of complex, non-Markovian quantum dynamics across diverse physical fields. [Phys. Rev. X 16, 020502] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Symplectic Rank of Non-Gaussian Quantum States
item.description:
📚 PRX Quantum
👤 Francesco A. Mele, Salvatore F.E. Oliviero, Varun Upreti, and Ulysse Chabaud
Author(s): Francesco A. Mele, Salvatore F.E. Oliviero, Varun Upreti, and Ulysse Chabaud The symplectic rank is shown to offer a unified and operationally meaningful measure of non-Gaussianity, dictating the minimum resources required to prepare, compress, and measure bosonic quantum states. [PRX Quantum 7, 020366] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026
